bull.b: Adoption by prominent centers such as Emory University Hospital and Tampa General Hospital offers clinical validation that could encourage other health systems to evaluate the platform.

Rapid customer uptake during the limited release across multiple procedure types indicates a potentially shorter sales cycle versus traditional surgical robots, supporting faster commercial momentum if scaled.
FDA clearance as a single-use remotely operated robotic system provides a distinct regulatory and product advantage that can strengthen market differentiation in peripheral endovascular procedures.

bear.b: Negative cash flow increases the probability of future capital raises, which could dilute existing investors if additional funding is required.
Available cash resources may be insufficient to sustain an aggressive commercial rollout without securing further financing, introducing uncertainty around the company’s runway.
Ongoing operating losses and negative cash flow from operations create financial pressure that may constrain flexibility in executing growth plans.
